title: RTL describes concurrent hardware
description: Every always block and continuous assignment evaluates in parallel each cycle; RTL is not sequential software statements.
body: Use when reasoning about why RTL behaves unlike software. Unlike imperative code, all always blocks and assign statements run concurrently and continuously; ordering within a clocked block matters only for blocking vs non-blocking semantics, not execution sequence across blocks. Do not read RTL top-to-bottom as steps. Actions: map each block to the hardware it infers (register, mux, adder) and verify exactly one driver per net. Validation signals include elaboration with no multi-driver errors. Risk boundary: assuming software-like ordering causes multi-driver and race bugs that synthesis or sim will flag — read the warnings.
